text.title: nodejs-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns the scaffold spec must never produce. Each has a symptom, a root cause, and a fix the agent can apply mechanically.

antipattern.symptom: Requests that throw return generic Express HTML error pages, never the JSON shape declared in the error_classes. Tests for 404 / 400 paths fail with HTML bodies.
antipattern.root-cause: errorHandler middleware was registered before app.use('/api', routes). Express middleware is order-sensitive: an earlier error handler never sees errors thrown by later middleware.
antipattern.fix: Move app.use(errorHandler) to the last line of createApp(). The output contract forbids middleware_order with errorHandler before routes — validate-nodejs-patterns.py catches this automatically.

antipattern.symptom: Test suite cannot run more than one test file in parallel; supertest reports EADDRINUSE; CI flakes randomly on port collisions.
antipattern.root-cause: createApp() calls app.listen(PORT) before returning. Every test file that imports createApp now binds a real socket, contending for the same port.
antipattern.fix: Split into app.ts (export createApp(): Express, no listen) and server.ts (const app = createApp(); app.listen(config.PORT)). Tests import createApp() only.

antipattern.symptom: Local dev runs fine; production crashes after the first request with "Cannot read property of undefined" inside a controller. No clear log line points at the missing env var.
antipattern.root-cause: process.env.SOMETHING is read inline inside a controller or service. The var was unset in the prod environment; the read returned undefined and was used as a string.
antipattern.fix: Move all process.env reads into config/env.ts, parse with Zod (or equivalent), export a frozen config object. Importing modules get typed, validated values; missing vars fail the process at boot.

antipattern.symptom: Process logs UnhandledPromiseRejection warnings; the response hangs until the client times out; the error handler never fires.
antipattern.root-cause: An async route handler threw, but it was registered as router.get('/', async (req, res) => {...}) without try/catch and without asyncHandler() wrapper. Express 4 does not forward async rejections to next().
antipattern.fix: Wrap every async handler in asyncHandler(fn) (Promise.resolve(fn(...)).catch(next)). Add an ESLint rule (no-misused-promises) to block raw async handlers on routers.

antipattern.symptom: Log aggregator (Loki / Datadog / CloudWatch) shows unstructured lines that cannot be filtered by requestId or statusCode. Some lines are missing entirely after a crash.
antipattern.root-cause: console.log is used inside handlers and services. Output is plain text on stdout, with no JSON shape and no flush-on-exit semantics.
antipattern.fix: Replace every console.* call with logger.info / logger.error from utils/logger.ts. Add a SIGTERM handler that calls logger.flush() before process.exit(0).
